title: Long-term effect of early-life stress from earthquake exposure on working memory in adulthood.
ehr_evidence: None; subjects were enrolled and tested using standardized cognitive tests (BVMT-R and HVLT-R).
summary: A study investigating the long-term effects of the 1976 Tangshan earthquake on the working memory of adults born in the region.
key_findings: Infant exposure to earthquake-related stress impairs visuospatial memory in adulthood.
Fetuses in the middle and late stages of development are more vulnerable to stress-induced damage.
Education level and early-life earthquake exposure are independent risk factors for reduced visuospatial memory.
population: 907 subjects born and raised in Tangshan, China
